message:
fix(agent/parser): only honor active-spinner override when spinner appears AFTER the most recent idle prompt

Operators polling `ntm --robot-is-working` on a Claude Code pane that
had just finished a long run (one real case: a 17-minute "thinking"
response) never saw the pane as idle, even after a fresh `❯ ` prompt
had been printed and the agent was clearly accepting input again. This
blocked the dispatcher from sending new marching orders to a perfectly
available pane.

Root cause — `internal/agent/parser.go :: detectIdle`:

The Claude idle branch previously had:

    if idleMatch && matchAnyRegex(lastLines, ccSpinnerActivePatterns) {
        return false
    }
    return idleMatch

i.e. ANY spinner anywhere in the tail of the buffer unconditionally
vetoed the idle verdict. That is correct when the agent is actively
thinking, but wrong when the spinner is a stale scrollback artifact
from the *previous* run: Claude Code's persistent status bar and the
"Crystallizing… (17m 2s · thinking)" line both persist in the
scrollback above a newly-printed `❯ ` prompt. Those stale spinner
lines then suppressed the idle signal indefinitely, even though a
fresh prompt sat below them, ready for input.

Fix — introduce a position-aware override:

    if idleMatch {
        lastIdle := lastLineIdxMatching(lastLines, ccIdlePatterns)
        lastSpin := lastLineIdxMatching(lastLines, ccSpinnerActivePatterns)
        if lastSpin > lastIdle {
            return false      // spinner is AFTER the prompt → working
        }
        return true           // prompt is AFTER the spinner → idle
    }
    return false

The spinner veto now only fires when the highest-index spinner line
(i.e. most recent in the scroll-back) sits strictly *after* the
highest-index idle-prompt line. If the idle prompt is the more recent
marker, the agent is idle and dispatchable.

Supporting change — `internal/agent/patterns.go`:

New `lastLineIdxMatching(text, patterns) int` helper that returns the
0-based line index of the last line in `text` that matches any of the
given regex patterns (or `-1` when nothing matches). It's a tight
single-pass scan and is used only by `detectIdle` today, but lives in
patterns.go alongside `matchAnyRegex` because it's the same shape of
utility and future parsers may need it.

Tests — `internal/agent/parser_test.go`:

Two regression tests, one for each direction of the ordering:

- `TestParser_Parse_Idle_Claude_StaleSpinnerAbovePrompt`: reconstructs
  the exact failure mode — a "✻ Crystallizing… (17m 2s · thinking)"
  line at the top of the tail, followed by a `❯ ` prompt, followed by
  the permanent `⏵⏵ bypass permissions on` status-bar fragment.
  Asserts `IsIdle=true`, `IsWorking=false`, and
  `RecommendSafeToRestart`. Before the fix this test fails exactly the
  way operators saw it fail in the field.

- `TestParser_Parse_Working_Claude_SpinnerBelowStalePrompt`: the
  inverse — the prompt appears first, followed by a fresh
  "Crystallizing… (3s · thinking)" spinner below it. Asserts
  `IsIdle=false`. This guards against a naive fix that just removes
  the spinner override entirely.

Commit message note: the existing spinner-working tests (they predate
this commit and cover the "spinner at end of buffer, no idle prompt"
case) continue to pass unchanged because the new ordering logic only
activates when `idleMatch` is true, which those fixtures don't satisfy.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
